prevalence.source: Urological Society of India context; regional stone belt surveys
prevalence.trend: increasing with heat exposure and dietary change

incidence.source: Urology cohort studies and hospital data

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; high acute pain and recurrence burden

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Renal colic with urinalysis; ultrasound or CT; metabolic work-up for recurrent stones

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Tens of millions (including recurrent stone formers)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; recurrence prevention limited by counseling and follow-up access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Ultrasound widely used; CT access limited in smaller centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Hydration, analgesia, alpha-blockers; ESWL/ureteroscopy/PCNL for larger stones; dietary prevention counseling
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited lithotripsy access in rural areas and delayed elective procedures
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing urology services and availability of minimally invasive procedures in urban centers
